title: HITL sources checkpoint + writer guidance
description: Add HITL 'sources' checkpoint after compressor: show sources/compressed summary and let user provide guidance that influences writer output.
acceptance_criteria:
- hitl_checkpoints includes sources triggers interrupt after compressor
- Resume accepts guidance content and stores it in state
- Writer includes guidance in prompt
- Tests cover interrupt+resume for sources checkpoint

title: Deepsearch tree: status page blocks on live preview
description: TreeExplorer.explore_branch_async awaits show_browser_status_page (sandbox_browser_sessions.run_sync). When browser_visualizer is previewing a slow URL, the single-worker browser executor is busy, so show_browser_status_page blocks and makes deepsearch appear stuck right after 'Prepared N queries'. Make status page best-effort (skip if preview in-flight, add timeout) and add progress logs around searches/summarization.
close_reason: Make deepsearch tree status-page/preview best-effort and add progress logs

title: Browser WS input actions (ack)
description: Add interactive input actions to /api/browser/{thread_id}/stream (mouse click + scroll + keyboard press/type + navigate) and always reply with ack {id, ok}. Include unit tests with dummy sandbox sessions.

title: Browser WS stream: ack unsupported actions
description: Ensure /api/browser/{thread_id}/stream always replies with an ack for unsupported or unknown action values (when client includes id), to prevent UI timeouts in Control mode. Add regression test.

title: HITL: interrupt status endpoint returns pending prompts
description: Implement /api/interrupt/{thread_id}/status by inspecting checkpointer pending_writes to detect hanging interrupts and return serialized prompts.
acceptance_criteria: - Status returns is_interrupted=true when pending interrupt exists\n- Response includes prompts compatible with SSE interrupt payload\n- Unit test creates a checkpoint with interrupt and verifies status

title: Integrate frontend stream protocol regression test into standard web test workflow
description: web/tests/chat-stream-protocol.test.ts currently runs only via ad-hoc node:test invocation. Integrate it into a standard web test script/CI so chunk-boundary regressions in the legacy 0:{json} stream parser are caught automatically.

title: Fix WS probe headers for websockets>=14
description: scripts/browser_stream_probe.py uses websockets.connect(extra_headers=...), but websockets 14 expects additional_headers. Add compatibility shim.

title: Fix live stream FPS display
description: Browser live stream dedupes identical base64 frames; FPS counter increments only on non-duplicate frames, so the UI can show 0 FPS and look stuck even when frames are being received. Show receive FPS (all frame messages) and optionally UI update rate separately.
close_reason: Implemented receive FPS + UI update rate display for live stream

title: Merge dependabot branches into main
description: Merge the remaining remote dependabot branches into main (GitHub Actions + Python deps + web deps). Resolve any conflicts, run local verification aligned with CI (python compileall + pytest; web pnpm lint/build where possible), then push the result. If main is protected, push an integration branch and open a PR instead.
acceptance_criteria: origin/main contains the dependabot updates; local verification commands run successfully or failures are reported with evidence; changes are pushed to the remote.

title: Fix browser live viewer 10fps streaming
description:
Context
- The browser live viewer requests max_fps=10 but currently only delivers about 1 FPS in real front-end measurements.
- The backend dedupe/keepalive path appears to throttle repeated frames too aggressively.

Acceptance Criteria
- Live browser stream honors max_fps=10 closely enough for the front-end FPS counter to approach 10 during active streaming.
- Idle/placeholder live mode no longer collapses to 1 FPS due solely to server-side throttling.
- Regression coverage exists for the repeated-frame emission path.
close_reason: Implemented backend live-stream pacing fix and verified 10 FPS in real browser smoke test

title: Fix simple deep-research latency and event flushing
description:
Context
- Deep research on simple factual prompts was taking minutes and often invoking unnecessary tree or agent tooling.
- Research tool events were not always flushed to the chat stream promptly.

Acceptance Criteria
- Simple factual deep prompts bypass heavy research execution.
- Search/quality events flush to the stream before graph completion.
- Regression tests cover the routing and stream flushing behavior.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with live deep-research smoke tests

title: Make advanced LangChain middlewares provider-safe on DeepSeek
description: Current DeepSeek runtime can safely use ToolRetryMiddleware, ToolCallLimitMiddleware, plan_steps, and task_list tools by default. However two advanced LangChain middlewares are still provider-sensitive: (1) LLMToolSelectorMiddleware is already gated off because DeepSeek's OpenAI-compatible gateway does not support the structured-output path LangChain expects; (2) TodoListMiddleware caused a live 400 error on 2026-03-06 ('messages[0]: invalid type: null, expected a string') when enabled under DeepSeek. Follow up by either adding provider-compatible implementations, backend-specific guards, or alternate selector/todo flows that work on DeepSeek without breaking agent mode.

title: Drive frontend model selector from backend public config
description: Frontend model dropdown is static; when backend is configured for only a subset of providers/models, selecting unsupported models causes upstream 400/500. Expose an allowlist (or supported providers/models) via /api/config/public or a new endpoint and have the frontend model selector use it.

title: Add browser stream websocket probe script
description: Add a standalone script that connects to /api/browser/{thread_id}/stream, starts streaming, and reports receive FPS vs unique-update FPS + first-frame latency. Used to debug 'Live view stuck' reports.
close_reason: Added browser stream WS probe script

title: BrowserViewer Control: touch scroll + better key mapping
description: Enhance BrowserViewer Control mode: support touch interactions (tap=click, drag=scroll), improve keyboard mapping (special keys + modifier chords), avoid duplicate paste handling. Add unit tests for key normalization utilities.

title: HITL checkpoints: plan/draft review interrupts + editable content UI
description: Make settings.hitl_checkpoints functional by adding explicit review nodes (plan/draft) that use langgraph interrupt() with payload; upgrade InterruptBanner to support instruction/content editing and resume with custom payload.
acceptance_criteria:
- When hitl_checkpoints includes plan/draft/final, graph emits interrupt with useful payload
- UI renders instruction+content and lets user edit + resume
- Backend resume supports these payloads (no regressions)
- Tests cover review node interrupt+resume

title: Reduce complex deep-research first-search latency
description:
Context
- Simple factual deep queries were fixed to bypass the heavy deep runner and now return quickly.
- Complex deep tree queries still took about 53s before the first search event in live validation on 2026-03-06.

Acceptance Criteria
- Identify the main contributors to the first-search delay for tree mode.
- Ship at least one measurable latency reduction or document why the remaining time is provider-bound.
- Add regression coverage for whichever fix is implemented.

title: Browser WS: add mouse move/down/up + viewport fallback
description: Extend /api/browser/{thread_id}/stream input handling: support mouse.move/down/up and coordinate mapping fallback (page.evaluate innerWidth/innerHeight) when viewport_size is missing. Add unit tests for each action and edge cases.

title: HITL tool approval: show args + reject/modify + resume schema
description: Enhance HITL tool approval flow: show pending tool calls (name + args), allow approve / reject / modify args, and resume graph with correct payload.
acceptance_criteria:
- Interrupt banner lists tool calls with args
- User can Approve (unchanged)
- User can Reject (resume with denial / stop)
- User can Modify tool args then Approve
- Backend validates & translates resume payload
- Tests cover payload + UI rendering

title: Reduce selector fallback latency on DeepSeek agent mode
description: After preferring json_mode for provider-safe tool selection, full agent mode now completes again on DeepSeek, but a live /api/chat run on 2026-03-06 still took about 205s for a three-capital prompt. During that run json_mode hit an incomplete chunked read once, then the selector fell back through function_calling/json_schema and finally proceeded with the full toolset. Follow up by reducing selector latency/retries for OpenAI-compatible backends and evaluating whether selector results should be cached across turns or selector transport errors should be retried more cheaply.

title: BrowserViewer: add Control mode (send WS input + show ack)
description: Implement frontend 'Control' toggle in BrowserViewer + useBrowserStream helpers to send mouse/scroll/keyboard/navigate actions to /api/browser/{thread_id}/stream, and surface ack status (success/failure) for UX + e2e.

title: Tighten agent-mode scope adherence for narrow comparison prompts
description: Live smoke on 2026-03-06 showed that agent mode still broadens some narrow comparison requests into unrelated dimensions. Example: the prompt 'Use current web search to compare the capitals of France and Germany in one sentence.' triggered three Tavily searches and returned a population comparison instead of simply comparing the capitals requested. Follow up by tightening the agent prompt and/or tool policy so comparison questions stay on the user-requested dimension and stop once the requested scope is satisfied.
close_reason: Fixed by routing narrow comparison prompts like 'compare the capitals of France and Germany in one sentence' through the fast single-search agent path, cleaning the search query, and constraining the fast-answer prompt to stay on the requested comparison dimension. Verified with targeted tests, full pytest, and live smoke returning the correct Paris/Berlin sentence in ~20.8s.

title: Tighten agent/web mode exact-answer formatting and cold-start search latency
close_reason: Fixed exact-answer output contracts and reduced web cold-start latency by limiting Tavily summarization fan-out.

title: Reduce agent-mode simple verification search latency
description: After fixing fallback routing and exact-output contracts, agent mode still spends ~45s on simple verification prompts because the model issues multiple Tavily searches for a single factual verification task. Follow up by constraining or pre-routing simple agent verification flows to a single-search path without sacrificing correctness.
close_reason: Fixed by adding a fast single-search verification path in agent_node for simple factual agent prompts; verified with targeted tests, full pytest, and live smoke reducing the simple verification case from ~45s previously to ~20.2s end-to-end with the fast path itself taking ~11.9s.
